mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending power, which directly reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the threshold, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and residential investment, which reduces aggregate economic activity and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict negative returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 50% over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expanding above its 2017 baseline indicates robust manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports near-term corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production above 100 correlates with negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of observations, or the predictive power vanishes during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 270 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of months when CPI exceeds 270, or the signal shows no predictive alpha after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts household income and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combination of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to predict positive returns, with negative returns occurring in >50% of cases over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects expanding economic output and corporate profit margins, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 98 signals a contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain bottlenecks, which reduces near-term earnings forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of instances when industrial production drops below 98, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during contractionary periods.
